content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 16, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 16, 2026, focused on hardware validation and architectural refinement for local inference. Efforts centered on verifying OpenVINO runtime capabilities for Intel hardware and addressing speculative decoding compatibility for M-RoPE model architectures.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Local Inference & Hardware Validation**
    *   Conducted validation passes for OpenVINO runtime on Apple Silicon and Intel hardware, confirming that while the backend foundation is present, specific Intel-based execution requires further environment configuration.
    *   Refined ASR wiring to prioritize OpenVINO Whisper on NPU-capable hosts, while maintaining fallback paths for fused ASR.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#7756](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7756): feat: add native macOS Swift shell.
*   [#7735](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7735): feat: add plugin-ezpath — pay-per-request DEX meta-router for Base mainnet.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   **OpenVINO Runtime Integration**
    *   [#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633): Closed the RFC regarding the OpenVINO runtime kernel for Intel hardware. The project has successfully integrated the backend foundation and ASR-side wiring, with further chat-runtime optimizations marked as out-of-scope for the current phase.

## NEW ISSUES
*   *None reported for this date.*

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   **Speculative Decoding Compatibility ([#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631))**
    *   Discussions confirmed that M-RoPE model architectures (like Qwen3.5) face strict position-validation requirements that conflict with the current speculative decoding harness.
    *   A mitigation patch was verified to prevent silent performance degradation, ensuring the system falls back to body-only inference when speculative decoding is unsupported. Hardware validation on Lunar Lake confirmed the fix prevents the previously observed acceptance collapse.
*   **Vulkan Bandwidth Efficiency ([#7638](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7638))**
    *   Investigations into the performance cliff for Qwen2.5-14B on Lunar Lake iGPUs continued. 
    *   Controlled benchmarks using various Vulkan toggles (async/graphics queue) indicated that the performance issue is not resolved by simple configuration changes, suggesting the need for deeper kernel-selection tuning in the underlying `ggml-vulkan` implementation.
